event: Early human hunter-gatherers occupy Andean sites such as Pikimachay Cave.
Hunter-gatherers at Guitarrero Cave begin cultivating early plants, marking incipient agriculture.
The monumental complex at Caral in the Supe Valley begins, becoming the oldest known city in the Americas.
The Chavin culture emerges, spreading a unifying religious art style centered on Chavin de Huantar.
The Nazca culture develops, later creating the massive geoglyphs known as the Nazca Lines.
The Moche civilization rises on the north coast, building adobe pyramids like the Huaca del Sol.
The Wari and Tiwanaku states expand, creating large Andean empires with road networks.
The Chimu kingdom emerges, eventually building its vast adobe capital of Chan Chan near modern Trujillo.
Pachacuti begins the rapid expansion of the Inca Empire (Tawantinsuyu) from its base at Cusco.
The Incas construct the royal estate of Machu Picchu in the Andes.
Francisco Pizarro captures the Inca emperor Atahualpa at Cajamarca, beginning the Spanish conquest.
The Spanish execute Atahualpa and seize Cusco, effectively toppling the Inca Empire.
Pizarro founds Lima, the City of Kings, as the new colonial capital.
Spain establishes the Viceroyalty of Peru, governing much of Spanish South America from Lima.
Viceroy Toledo captures and executes Tupac Amaru, the last Inca ruler of Vilcabamba.
Tupac Amaru II leads a massive indigenous rebellion against Spanish colonial rule.
Spanish authorities defeat and execute Tupac Amaru II, ending the great Andean uprising.
Jose de San Martin declares Peru's independence from Spain in Lima.
The patriot victory at the Battle of Ayacucho secures Peruvian and South American independence from Spain.
Andres de Santa Cruz forms the Peru-Bolivian Confederation, which is dissolved in 1839.
Ramon Castilla begins his presidency, ushering in stability funded by the lucrative guano boom.
President Castilla abolishes slavery and the indigenous head tax in Peru.
Peru defeats a Spanish fleet at the Battle of Callao during the Chincha Islands War.
The War of the Pacific begins as Peru and Bolivia fight Chile over nitrate-rich territory.
The Treaty of Ancon ends the War of the Pacific, with Peru ceding the province of Tarapaca to Chile.
Nicolas de Pierola takes power, beginning the 'Aristocratic Republic' era of oligarchic rule.
Hiram Bingham brings international attention to the Inca site of Machu Picchu.
Victor Raul Haya de la Torre founds the APRA, a major populist political movement.
Peru and Chile sign the Treaty of Lima, returning Tacna to Peru while Arica remains Chilean.
Peru fights a brief border war with Ecuador, later settled by the 1942 Rio Protocol.
Peru becomes a founding member of the United Nations.
General Manuel Odria seizes power in a coup, beginning an authoritarian eight-year rule.
General Juan Velasco Alvarado leads a military coup and launches sweeping land reform and nationalizations.
Democracy returns with Fernando Belaunde's election, as the Maoist Shining Path begins its insurgency.
Alan Garcia becomes Peru's first APRA president amid soaring inflation and economic crisis.
Alberto Fujimori wins the presidency and imposes drastic free-market 'Fujishock' economic reforms.
Fujimori carries out a self-coup dissolving Congress, and forces capture Shining Path leader Abimael Guzman.
Peru and Ecuador fight the brief Cenepa War over their disputed Amazonian border.
MRTA guerrillas seize the Japanese ambassador's residence in Lima, holding hostages for months.
Peru and Ecuador sign the Brasilia Presidential Act, permanently resolving their border dispute.
Fujimori flees to Japan and resigns amid a vast corruption scandal exposed by the Vladivideos.
Alejandro Toledo becomes Peru's first president of indigenous Andean descent in modern times.
The Truth and Reconciliation Commission reports that roughly 70,000 died in the internal conflict.
Former president Alberto Fujimori is convicted of human rights abuses and sentenced to prison.
Ollanta Humala wins the presidency on a platform of inclusive growth amid a mining boom.
Pedro Pablo Kuczynski narrowly defeats Keiko Fujimori to become president.
Kuczynski resigns over corruption allegations linked to the Odebrecht scandal.
Peru endures political turmoil with three presidents in one week amid the COVID-19 pandemic.
President Pedro Castillo is impeached and arrested after trying to dissolve Congress, and Dina Boluarte takes office.
